Đã hoàn thành theo đúng đoạn `@.txt` (dòng 6–12): phân tích ba góc độ và gộp vào **`OVERVIEW.md`** ở thư mục gốc repo, có **nhiều sơ đồ Mermaid** (kiến trúc lớp, sequence fetch→parse, luồng Spider/crawl, entry CLI/MCP/thư viện).

### Nội dung chính trong `OVERVIEW.md`

1. **Kiến trúc**: layered (engines → fetchers → parser; spiders tách scheduler/engine/session), pattern strategy/pluggable storage cho adaptive, lazy imports, extras tùy chọn.
2. **Lập trình viên**: bảng thư mục quan trọng, phụ thuộc cốt lõi vs optional, file đáng chú ý (`parser.py`, `spiders/engine.py`, `cli.py`), gợi ý bảo trì.
3. **Sản phẩm**: mục tiêu (scraping hiện đại + adaptive + scale), bảng tính năng, user flows (lib / spider / CLI / MCP).

Repo này là **thư viện Python Scrapling** (web scraping), **không** có tính năng faceswap — các đoạn faceswap trong `@.txt` là prompt khác, không áp dụng cho repo này.

File: `E:\github\Scrapling\OVERVIEW.md`. Nếu bạn muốn thêm mục tiêu riêng (ví dụ chỉ MCP hoặc chỉ spider), có thể nói để bổ sung một mục trong cùng file.

Đã tổng hợp vào file **`ONBOARDING.md`** ở gốc repo (`E:\github\Scrapling\ONBOARDING.md`). Nội dung gồm:

1. **Chức năng chính** — thư viện scraping: fetch (HTTP / dynamic / stealth), parse + adaptive, spider crawl, CLI và MCP.
2. **Cấu trúc thư mục** — bảng mô tả `scrapling/`, `tests/`, `docs/`, `agent-skill/`, CI, `pyproject.toml`, Docker.
3. **Luồng dữ liệu** — làm rõ **không có REST API mặc định**; ba đường vào: **Python API** → fetchers → engines → parser/storage; **CLI** (`scrapling.cli:main`); **MCP** (`scrapling mcp` → `core.ai` / FastMCP → cùng fetchers). Thêm luồng **Spider** (scheduler → engine → session → parse).
4. **Stack** — bảng từ `pyproject.toml` (core deps + optional fetchers / ai / shell).
5. **Mermaid** — sơ đồ ba đường vào và sơ đồ spider crawl.

File **`OVERVIEW.md`** (đã có trước đó) đi sâu kiến trúc/PM hơn; **`ONBOARDING.md`** tập trung góc nhìn “thành viên mới” và trả lời đúng câu hỏi về “endpoint” (ở đây là entry **CLI/MCP/process**, không phải HTTP API của app).

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /d E:\github\Scrapling && python -c "import ast, os; roots=['scrapling'];
for r in roots:
  for dp, dn, fn in os.walk(r):
    for f in fn:
      if f.endswith('.py'):
        p=os.path.join(dp,f)
        try:
          with open(p,encoding='utf-8') as fh: tree=ast.parse(fh.read())
        except: continue
        for node in ast.walk(tree):
          if isinstance(node, ast.FunctionDef):
            n=sum(1 for _ in ast.walk(node))
            if n>400:
              print(f'{p}:{node.name} approx_nodes={n}')
"
</summary>

      if f.endswith('.py'):
        p=os.path.join(dp,f)
        try:
          with open(p,encoding='utf-8') as fh: tree=ast.parse(fh.read())
        except: continue
        for node in ast.walk(tree):
          if isinstance(node, ast.FunctionDef):
            n=sum(1 for _ in ast.walk(node))
            if n>350:
              print(f'{p}:{node.name} approx_nodes={n}')
"

            "text": "        verify: bool = True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 670,
            "text": "        cert: Optional[str | Tuple[str, str]] = None,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 671,
            "text": "        selector_config: Optional[Dict] = None,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 672,
            "text": "        proxy_rotator: Optional[ProxyRotator] = None,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 673,
            "text": "    ):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 674,
            "text": "        \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 675,
            "text": "        :param impersonate: Browser version to impersonate. Can be a single browser string or a list of browser strings for random selection. (Default: latest available Chrome version)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 676,
            "text": "        :param http3: Whether to use HTTP3. Defaults to False. It might be problematic if used it with &#96;impersonate&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 677,
            "text": "        :param stealthy_headers: If enabled (default), it creates and adds real browser headers. It also sets a Google referer header."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 678,
            "text": "        :param proxies: Dict of proxies to use. Format: {\"http\": proxy_url, \"https\": proxy_url}."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 679,
            "text": "        :param proxy: Proxy URL to use. Format: \"http://username:password@localhost:8030\"."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 680,
            "text": "                     Cannot be used together with the &#96;proxies&#96; parameter."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 681,
            "text": "        :param proxy_auth: HTTP basic auth for proxy, tuple of (username, password)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 682,
            "text": "        :param timeout: Number of seconds to wait before timing out."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 683,
            "text": "        :param headers: Headers to include in the session with every request."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 684,
            "text": "        :param retries: Number of retry attempts. Defaults to 3."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 685,
            "text": "        :param retry_delay: Number of seconds to wait between retry attempts. Defaults to 1 second."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 686,
            "text": "        :param follow_redirects: Whether to follow redirects. Defaults to \"safe\", which follows redirects but rejects those targeting internal/private IPs (SSRF protection). Pass True to follow all redirects without restriction."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 687,
            "text": "        :param max_redirects: Maximum number of redirects. Default 30, use -1 for unlimited."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 688,
            "text": "        :param verify: Whether to verify HTTPS certificates. Defaults to True."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 689,
            "text": "        :param cert: Tuple of (cert, key) filenames for the client certificate."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 690,
            "text": "        :param selector_config: Arguments passed when creating the final Selector class."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 691,
            "text": "        :param proxy_rotator: A ProxyRotator instance for automatic proxy rotation."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 692,
            "text": "        \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 693,
            "text": "        self._default_impersonate: ImpersonateType = impersonate"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 694,
            "text": "        self._stealth = stealthy_headers"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 695,
            "text": "        self._default_proxies = proxies or {}"

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 498,
            "text": "class StealthySessionMixin(BaseSessionMixin):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 499,
            "text": "    def __validate__(self, **params):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 500,
            "text": "        self._config = self.__validate_routine__(params, model=StealthConfig)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 501,
            "text": "        self._context_options.update("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 502,
            "text": "            {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 503,
            "text": "                \"is_mobile\": False,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 504,
            "text": "                \"has_touch\": False,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 505,
            "text": "                # I'm thinking about disabling it to rest from all Service Workers' headache, but let's keep it as it is for now"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 506,
            "text": "                \"service_workers\": \"allow\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 507,
            "text": "                \"ignore_https_errors\": True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 508,
            "text": "                \"screen\": {\"width\": 1920, \"height\": 1080},"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 509,
            "text": "                \"viewport\": {\"width\": 1920, \"height\": 1080},"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 510,
            "text": "                \"permissions\": [\"geolocation\", \"notifications\"],"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 511,
            "text": "            }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 512,
            "text": "        )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 513,
            "text": "        self.__generate_stealth_options()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 514
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 515,
            "text": "    def __generate_stealth_options(self) -\u003e None:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 516,
            "text": "        config = cast(StealthConfig, self._config)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 517,
            "text": "        flags: Tuple[str, ...] = tuple()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 518,
            "text": "        if not config.cdp_url:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 519,
            "text": "            flags = tuple(DEFAULT_ARGS) + tuple(STEALTH_ARGS)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 520
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 521,
            "text": "            if config.block_webrtc:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 522,
            "text": "                flags += ("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 523,
            "text": "                    \"--webrtc-ip-handling-policy=disable_non_proxied_udp\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 524,
            "text": "                    \"--force-webrtc-ip-handling-policy\",  # Ensures the policy is enforced"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 525,
            "text": "                )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 526,
            "text": "            if not config.allow_webgl:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 527,
            "text": "                flags += ("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 528,
            "text": "                    \"--disable-webgl\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 529,
            "text": "                    \"--disable-webgl-image-chromium\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 530,
            "text": "                    \"--disable-webgl2\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 531,

            "lineNumber": 534
          },

            "text": "        super(StealthySessionMixin, self).__generate_options__(flags)"

          {
            "isSignature": true,

            "lineNumber": 588,
            "text": "async def stealthy_fetch("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 589,
            "text": "        self,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 590,
            "text": "        url: str,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 591,
            "text": "        extraction_type: extraction_types = \"markdown\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 592,
            "text": "        css_selector: Optional[str] = None,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 593,
            "text": "        main_content_only: bool = True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 594,
            "text": "        headless: bool = True,  # noqa: F821"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 595,
            "text": "        google_search: bool = True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 596,
            "text": "        real_chrome: bool = False,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 597,
            "text": "        wait: int | float = 0,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 598,
            "text": "        proxy: Optional[str | Dict[str, str]] = None,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 599,
            "text": "        timezone_id: str | None = None,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 600,
            "text": "        locale: str | None = None,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 601,
            "text": "        extra_headers: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 602,
            "text": "        useragent: Optional[str] = None,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 603,
            "text": "        hide_canvas: bool = False,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 604,
            "text": "        cdp_url: Optional[str] = None,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 605,
            "text": "        timeout: int | float = 30000,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 606,
            "text": "        disable_resources: bool = False,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 607,
            "text": "        wait_selector: Optional[str] = None,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 608,
            "text": "        cookies: Sequence[SetCookieParam] | None = None,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 609,
            "text": "        network_idle: bool = False,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 610,
            "text": "        wait_selector_state: SelectorWaitStates = \"attached\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 611,
            "text": "        block_webrtc: bool = False,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 612,
            "text": "        allow_webgl: bool = True,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 613,
            "text": "        solve_cloudflare: bool = False,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 614,
            "text": "        additional_args: Optional[Dict] = None,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 615,
            "text": "        session_id: Optional[str] = None,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 616,
            "text": "    ) -\u003e ResponseModel:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 617,
            "text": "\"\"\"Use the stealthy fetcher to fetch a URL and return a structured output of the result."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 618,
            "text": "        Note: This is the only suitable fetcher for high protection levels."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 619,
            "text": "        Note: If the &#96;css_selector&#96; resolves to more than one element, all the elements will be returned."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 620,
            "text": "        Note: If a &#96;session_id&#96; is provided (from open_session), the browser session will be reused instead of creating a new one."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 621,
            "text": "            When using a session, browser-level params (headless, proxy, locale, etc.) are ignored since they were set at session creation time."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 622
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 623,
            "text": "        :param url: The URL to request."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 624,
            "text": "        :param extraction_type: The type of content to extract from the page. Defaults to \"markdown\". Options are:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 625,
            "text": "            - Markdown will convert the page content to Markdown format."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 626,
            "text": "            - HTML will return the raw HTML content of the page."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 627,
            "text": "            - Text will return the text content of the page."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 628,
            "text": "        :param css_selector: CSS selector to extract the content from the page. If main_content_only is True, then it will be executed on the main content of the page. Defaults to None."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 629,
            "text": "        :param main_content_only: Whether to extract only the main content of the page. Defaults to True. The main content here is the data inside the &#96;\u003cbody\u003e&#96; tag."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 630,
            "text": "        :param headless: Run the browser in headless/hidden (default), or headful/visible mode."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 631,
            "text": "        :param disable_resources: Drop requests for unnecessary resources for a speed boost."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 632,
            "text": "            Requests dropped are of type &#96;font&#96;, &#96;image&#96;, &#96;media&#96;, &#96;beacon&#96;, &#96;object&#96;, &#96;imageset&#96;, &#96;texttrack&#96;, &#96;websocket&#96;, &#96;csp_report&#96;, and &#96;stylesheet&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 633,
            "text": "        :param useragent: Pass a useragent string to be used. Otherwise the fetcher will generate a real Useragent of the same browser and use it."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 634,
            "text": "        :param cookies: Set cookies for the next request."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 635,
            "text": "        :param solve_cloudflare: Solves all types of the Cloudflare's Turnstile/Interstitial challenges before returning the response to you."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 636,
            "text": "        :param allow_webgl: Enabled by default. Disabling WebGL is not recommended as many WAFs now check if WebGL is enabled."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 637,
            "text": "        :param network_idle: Wait for the page until there are no network connections for at least 500 ms."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 638,
            "text": "        :param wait: The time (milliseconds) the fetcher will wait after everything finishes before closing the page and returning the &#96; Response &#96; object."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 639,
            "text": "        :param timeout: The timeout in milliseconds that is used in all operations and waits through the page. The default is 30,000"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 640,
            "text": "        :param wait_selector: Wait for a specific CSS selector to be in a specific state."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 641,
            "text": "        :param timezone_id: Changes the timezone of the browser. Defaults to the system timezone."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 642,
            "text": "        :param locale: Specify user locale, for example, &#96;en-GB&#96;, &#96;de-DE&#96;, etc. Locale will affect navigator.language value, Accept-Language request header value as well as number and date formatting"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 643,
            "text": "            rules. Defaults to the system default locale."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 644,
            "text": "        :param wait_selector_state: The state to wait for the selector given with &#96;wait_selector&#96;. The default state is &#96;attached&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 645,
            "text": "        :param real_chrome: If you have a Chrome browser installed on your device, enable this, and the Fetcher will launch an instance of your browser and use it."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 646,
            "text": "        :param hide_canvas: Add random noise to canvas operations to prevent fingerprinting."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 647,
            "text": "        :param block_webrtc: Forces WebRTC to respect proxy settings to prevent local IP address leak."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 648,
            "text": "        :param cdp_url: Instead of launching a new browser instance, connect to this CDP URL to control real browsers through CDP."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 649,
            "text": "        :param google_search: Enabled by default, Scrapling will set a Google referer header."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 650,
            "text": "        :param extra_headers: A dictionary of extra headers to add to the request. _The referer set by &#96;google_search&#96; takes priority over the referer set here if used together._"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 651,
            "text": "        :param proxy: The proxy to be used with requests, it can be a string or a dictionary with the keys 'server', 'username', and 'password' only."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 652,
            "text": "        :param additional_args: Additional arguments to be passed to Playwright's context as additional settings, and it takes higher priority than Scrapling's settings."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 653,
            "text": "        :param session_id: Optional session ID from open_session. If provided, reuses the existing browser session instead of creating a new one."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 654,
            "text": "        \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 655,
            "text": "        results = await self.bulk_stealthy_fetch("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 656,
            "text": "            urls=[url],"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 657,
            "text": "            extraction_type=extraction_type,"
          }
        ],

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96,
            "text": "    @staticmethod"
          },

            "lineNumber": 97,
            "text": "    def _get_param(kwargs: Dict, key: str, default: Any) -\u003e Any:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 98,
            "text": "        \"\"\"Get parameter from kwargs if present, otherwise return default.\"\"\""
          },

            "text": "        return kwargs[key] if key in kwargs else default"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 100
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 101,
            "text": "    def _merge_request_args(self, **method_kwargs) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 102,
            "text": "        \"\"\"Merge request-specific arguments with default session arguments.\"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 103,
            "text": "        url = method_kwargs.pop(\"url\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 104
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 105,
            "text": "        # Get parameters from kwargs or use defaults"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 106,
            "text": "        impersonate = self._get_param(method_kwargs, \"impersonate\", self._default_impersonate)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 107,
            "text": "        impersonate = _select_random_browser(impersonate)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 108,
            "text": "        http3_enabled = self._get_param(method_kwargs, \"http3\", self._default_http3)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 109,
            "text": "        stealth = self._get_param(method_kwargs, \"stealth\", self._stealth)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 110
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 111,
            "text": "        final_args = {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 112,
            "text": "            \"url\": url,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 113,
            "text": "            # Curl automatically generates the suitable browser headers when you use &#96;impersonate&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 114,
            "text": "            \"headers\": self._headers_job("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 115,
            "text": "                url,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 116,
            "text": "                self._get_param(method_kwargs, \"headers\", self._default_headers),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 117,
            "text": "                stealth,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118,
            "text": "                bool(impersonate),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 119,
            "text": "            ),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120,
            "text": "            \"proxies\": self._get_param(method_kwargs, \"proxies\", self._default_proxies),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 121,

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "class StealthySession(SyncSession, StealthySessionMixin):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 181
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 182
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 183,
            "text": "    def fetch(self, url: str, **kwargs: Unpack[StealthFetchParams]) -\u003e Response:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 184,
            "text": "        \"\"\"Opens up the browser and do your request based on your chosen options."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 185
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 186,
            "text": "        :param url: The Target url."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 187,
            "text": "        :param google_search: Enabled by default, Scrapling will set a Google referer header."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 188,
            "text": "        :param timeout: The timeout in milliseconds that is used in all operations and waits through the page. The default is 30,000"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 189,
            "text": "        :param wait: The time (milliseconds) the fetcher will wait after everything finishes before closing the page and returning the &#96; Response &#96; object."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 190,
            "text": "        :param page_action: Added for automation. A function that takes the &#96;page&#96; object and does the automation you need."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 191,
            "text": "        :param extra_headers: A dictionary of extra headers to add to the request. _The referer set by &#96;google_search&#96; takes priority over the referer set here if used together._"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 192,
            "text": "        :param disable_resources: Drop requests for unnecessary resources for a speed boost."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 193,
            "text": "            Requests dropped are of type &#96;font&#96;, &#96;image&#96;, &#96;media&#96;, &#96;beacon&#96;, &#96;object&#96;, &#96;imageset&#96;, &#96;texttrack&#96;, &#96;websocket&#96;, &#96;csp_report&#96;, and &#96;stylesheet&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 194,
            "text": "        :param blocked_domains: A set of domain names to block requests to. Subdomains are also matched (e.g., &#96;&#96;\"example.com\"&#96;&#96; blocks &#96;&#96;\"sub.example.com\"&#96;&#96; too)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 195,
            "text": "        :param wait_selector: Wait for a specific CSS selector to be in a specific state."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 196,
            "text": "        :param wait_selector_state: The state to wait for the selector given with &#96;wait_selector&#96;. The default state is &#96;attached&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 197,
            "text": "        :param network_idle: Wait for the page until there are no network connections for at least 500 ms."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 198,
            "text": "        :param load_dom: Enabled by default, wait for all JavaScript on page(s) to fully load and execute."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 199,
            "text": "        :param solve_cloudflare: Solves all types of the Cloudflare's Turnstile/Interstitial challenges before returning the response to you."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 200,
            "text": "        :param selector_config: The arguments that will be passed in the end while creating the final Selector's class."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 201,
            "text": "        :param proxy: Static proxy to override rotator and session proxy. A new browser context will be created and used with it."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 202,
            "text": "        :return: A &#96;Response&#96; object."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 203,
            "text": "        \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 204,
            "text": "        static_proxy = kwargs.pop(\"proxy\", None)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 205
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 206,
            "text": "        params = _validate(kwargs, self, StealthConfig)"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 291
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 21
          }
        },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 207,
            "text": "class AsyncDynamicSession(AsyncSession, DynamicSessionMixin):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 281,
            "text": "async def fetch(self, url: str, **kwargs: Unpack[PlaywrightFetchParams]) -\u003e Response:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 300,
            "text": "\"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 301,
            "text": "        static_proxy = kwargs.pop(\"proxy\", None)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 302
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 303,
            "text": "        params = _validate(kwargs, self, PlaywrightConfig)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 304
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 305,
            "text": "        if not self._is_alive:  # pragma: no cover"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 306,
            "text": "            raise RuntimeError(\"Context manager has been closed\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 307
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 308,
            "text": "        request_headers_keys = {h.lower() for h in params.extra_headers.keys()} if params.extra_headers else set()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 309,
            "text": "        referer = ("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 310,
            "text": "            \"https://www.google.com/\" if (params.google_search and \"referer\" not in request_headers_keys) else None"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 311,
            "text": "        )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 312
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 313,
            "text": "        for attempt in range(self._config.retries):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 314,
            "text": "            proxy: Optional[ProxyType] = None"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 315,
            "text": "            if self._config.proxy_rotator and static_proxy is None:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 316,
            "text": "                proxy = self._config.proxy_rotator.get_proxy()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 317,
            "text": "            else:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 318,
            "text": "                proxy = static_proxy"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 319
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 320,
            "text": "            async with self._page_generator("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 321,
            "text": "                params.timeout, params.extra_headers, params.disable_resources, proxy, params.blocked_domains"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 322,
            "text": "            ) as page_info:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 323,
            "text": "                final_response: List = [None]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 324,
            "text": "                xhr_captured: List = []"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 384
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 206
          }

Đã hoàn thành đợt review tĩnh và ghi vào **`CODE_REVIEW.md`** (`E:\github\Scrapling\CODE_REVIEW.md`).

### Tóm tắt nội dung

| Hạng mục | Điểm chính |
|----------|------------|
| **Code quality** | Trùng docstring (đặc biệt `engines/static.py`, MCP tools); các hàm rất lớn: `CurlParser.parse`, `Selector.find_all`, `fetch` trong stealth/controllers; gợi ý tách bước / factory. |
| **Security** | SQLite dùng `?` — không thấy SQL injection kiểu nối chuỗi. **`pickle.loads` trên `checkpoint.pkl`** là rủi ro deserialize nếu file bị thay (đã phản ánh trong `.bandit.yml`). Redirect có nhánh **safe** chống SSRF; MCP vẫn phụ thuộc chính sách triển khai URL. Có lớp giảm prompt injection trong `shell.py`. |
| **Performance** | `orjson`, `lxml`, WAL phù hợp; bottleneck tiềm ẩn: **adaptive similarity** trên DOM lớn, **cold start browser**, ghi checkpoint định kỳ. |
| **Python best practices** | Typing, `pyproject.toml`, extras; Bandit bỏ qua một số rule — nên ghi rõ lý do trong quy trình. |

File có **3 sơ đồ Mermaid**: chiều review, mô hình đe dọa (pickle/MCP), luồng fetch→parse và chi phí.

Bạn có muốn mình chạy **`pip install bandit` rồi `bandit -r scrapling`** trong repo để bổ sung output thực tế vào cuối file không?

            "text": "class Selector(SelectorsGeneration):"

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 870,
            "text": "    @staticmethod"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 871,
            "text": "    def __calculate_dict_diff(dict1: Dict, dict2: Dict) -\u003e float:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 872,
            "text": "        \"\"\"Used internally to calculate similarity between two dictionaries as SequenceMatcher doesn't accept dictionaries\"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 873,
            "text": "        score = SequenceMatcher(None, tuple(dict1.keys()), tuple(dict2.keys())).ratio() * 0.5"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 874,
            "text": "        score += SequenceMatcher(None, tuple(dict1.values()), tuple(dict2.values())).ratio() * 0.5"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 875,
            "text": "        return score"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 876
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 877,
            "text": "    def save(self, element: HtmlElement, identifier: str) -\u003e None:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 878,
            "text": "        \"\"\"Saves the element's unique properties to the storage for retrieval and relocation later"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 879
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 880,
            "text": "        :param element: The element itself that we want to save to storage, it can be a &#96; Selector &#96; or pure &#96; HtmlElement &#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 881,
            "text": "        :param identifier: This is the identifier that will be used to retrieve the element later from the storage. See"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 882,
            "text": "            the docs for more info."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 883,
            "text": "        \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 884,
            "text": "        if self.__adaptive_enabled and self._storage:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 885,
            "text": "            target_element: Any = element"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 886,
            "text": "            if isinstance(target_element, self.__class__):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 887,
            "text": "                target_element = target_element._root"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 888
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 889,
            "text": "            if self._is_text_node(target_element):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 890,
            "text": "                target_element = target_element.getparent()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 891
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 892,
            "text": "            self._storage.save(target_element, identifier)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 893,
            "text": "        else:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 894,
            "text": "            raise RuntimeError("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 895,
            "text": "                \"Can't use &#96;adaptive&#96; features while it's disabled globally, you have to start a new class instance.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 896,
            "text": "            )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 897
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 898,
            "text": "    def retrieve(self, identifier: str) -\u003e Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:"
          },

          },

          },

      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 64,
            "text": "class Selector(SelectorsGeneration):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 968,
            "text": "def __are_alike("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 969,
            "text": "        self,"

            "lineNumber": 970,
            "text": "        original: HtmlElement,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 971,
            "text": "        original_attributes: Dict,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 972,
            "text": "        candidate: HtmlElement,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 973,
            "text": "        ignore_attributes: List | Tuple,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 974,
            "text": "        similarity_threshold: float,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 975,
            "text": "        match_text: bool = False,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 976,
            "text": "    ) -\u003e bool:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1006
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1007,
            "text": "        return False"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1008
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1009,
            "text": "    def find_similar("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1010,
            "text": "        self,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1011,
            "text": "        similarity_threshold: float = 0.2,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1012,
            "text": "        ignore_attributes: List | Tuple = ("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1013,
            "text": "            \"href\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1014,
            "text": "            \"src\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1015,
            "text": "        ),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1016,
            "text": "        match_text: bool = False,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1017,
            "text": "    ) -\u003e \"Selectors\":"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1018,
            "text": "        \"\"\"Find elements that are in the same tree depth in the page with the same tag name and same parent tag etc..."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1019,
            "text": "        then return the ones that match the current element attributes with a percentage higher than the input threshold."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1020
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1021,
            "text": "        This function is inspired by AutoScraper and made for cases where you, for example, found a product div inside"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1022,
            "text": "        a products-list container and want to find other products using that element as a starting point EXCEPT"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1023,
            "text": "        this function works in any case without depending on the element type."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1024
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1025,
            "text": "        :param similarity_threshold: The percentage to use while comparing element attributes."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1026,
            "text": "            Note: Elements found before attributes matching/comparison will be sharing the same depth, same tag name,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1027,
            "text": "            same parent tag name, and same grand parent tag name. So they are 99% likely to be correct unless you are"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1028,
            "text": "            extremely unlucky, then attributes matching comes into play, so don't play with this number unless"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1029,
            "text": "            you are getting the results you don't want."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1030,
            "text": "            Also, if the current element doesn't have attributes and the similar element as well, then it's a 100% match."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1031,
            "text": "        :param ignore_attributes: Attribute names passed will be ignored while matching the attributes in the last step."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1032,
            "text": "            The default value is to ignore &#96;href&#96; and &#96;src&#96; as URLs can change a lot between elements, so it's unreliable"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1033,
            "text": "        :param match_text: If True, element text content will be taken into calculation while matching."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1034,
            "text": "            Not recommended to use in normal cases, but it depends."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1035
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1036,
            "text": "        :return: A &#96;&#96;Selectors&#96;&#96; container of &#96;&#96;Selector&#96;&#96; objects or empty list"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1037,
            "text": "        \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1038,
            "text": "        if self._is_text_node(self._root):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1039,
            "text": "            return Selectors()"
          }
        ],

            "text": "        **kwargs: Any,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 632,
            "text": "    ) -\u003e \"Selectors\":"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 633,
            "text": "        \"\"\"Search the current tree with XPath selectors"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 634
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 635,
            "text": "        **Important:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 636,
            "text": "        It's recommended to use the identifier argument if you plan to use a different selector later"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 637,
            "text": "        and want to relocate the same element(s)**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 638
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 639,
            "text": "         Note: **Additional keyword arguments will be passed as XPath variables in the XPath expression!**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 640
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 641,
            "text": "        :param selector: The XPath selector to be used."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 642,
            "text": "        :param adaptive: Enabled will make the function try to relocate the element if it was 'saved' before"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 643,
            "text": "        :param identifier: A string that will be used to save/retrieve element's data in adaptive,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 644,
            "text": "         otherwise the selector will be used."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 645,
            "text": "        :param auto_save: Automatically save new elements for &#96;adaptive&#96; later"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 646,
            "text": "        :param percentage: The minimum percentage to accept while &#96;adaptive&#96; is working and not going lower than that."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 647,
            "text": "         Be aware that the percentage calculation depends solely on the page structure, so don't play with this"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 648,
            "text": "         number unless you must know what you are doing!"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 649
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 650,
            "text": "        :return: &#96;Selectors&#96; class."
          },
          {

            "text": "        \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 652,
            "text": "        if self._is_text_node(self._root):"

          {
            "lineNumber": 653,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 654
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 655,
            "text": "        try:"

            "text": "class Selector(SelectorsGeneration):"

            "text": "\"\"\""

          {
            "lineNumber": 1038,
            "text": "        if self._is_text_node(self._root):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1039,
            "text": "            return Selectors()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1040
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1041,
            "text": "        # We will use the elements' root from now on to get the speed boost of using Lxml directly"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1042,
            "text": "        root = self._root"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1043,
            "text": "        similar_elements = list()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1044
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1045,
            "text": "        current_depth = len(list(root.iterancestors()))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1046,
            "text": "        target_attrs = self.__get_attributes(root, ignore_attributes) if ignore_attributes else root.attrib"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1047
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1048,
            "text": "        path_parts = [self.tag]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1049,
            "text": "        if (parent := root.getparent()) is not None:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1050,
            "text": "            path_parts.insert(0, parent.tag)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1051,
            "text": "            if (grandparent := parent.getparent()) is not None:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1052,
            "text": "                path_parts.insert(0, grandparent.tag)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1053
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1054,
            "text": "        xpath_path = \"//{}\".format(\"/\".join(path_parts))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1055,
            "text": "        potential_matches = root.xpath(f\"{xpath_path}[count(ancestor::*) = {current_depth}]\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1056
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1057,
            "text": "        for potential_match in potential_matches:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1058,
            "text": "            if potential_match != root and self.__are_alike("

          {
            "lineNumber": 1059,
            "text": "                root,"

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1068,
            "text": "        return Selectors(map(self.__element_convertor, similar_elements))"

            "lineNumber": 1069
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1070,
            "text": "    @overload"

            "text": "    ) -\u003e \"Selector\": ..."

          },
          {

            "text": "    @overload"

          {
            "lineNumber": 1081,
            "text": "    def find_by_text("

          {
            "lineNumber": 1082,
            "text": "        self,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1083,
            "text": "        text: str,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1084,
            "text": "        first_match: Literal[False],"
          },

          },
          {

          },
          {

          },

            "text": "def find_all("

          },

          },
          {

            "text": "        return results"

            "lineNumber": 787
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 788,
            "text": "    def find("

          {
            "lineNumber": 789,

          },

            "lineNumber": 790,
            "text": "        *args: str | Iterable[str] | Pattern | Callable | Dict[str, str],"
          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 792,
            "text": "    ) -\u003e Optional[\"Selector\"]:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 793,
            "text": "        \"\"\"Find elements by filters of your creations for ease, then return the first result. Otherwise return &#96;None&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 794
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 795,
            "text": "        :param args: Tag name(s), iterable of tag names, regex patterns, function, or a dictionary of elements' attributes. Leave empty for selecting all."

          {
            "lineNumber": 796,
            "text": "        :param kwargs: The attributes you want to filter elements based on it."
          },

          },
          {

            "text": "        \"\"\""
          },
          {

            "text": "        return None"

            "lineNumber": 802
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 803,
            "text": "    def __calculate_similarity_score(self, original: Dict, candidate: HtmlElement) -\u003e float:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 804,
            "text": "        \"\"\"Used internally to calculate a score that shows how a candidate element similar to the original one"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 805
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 806,
            "text": "        :param original: The original element in the form of the dictionary generated from &#96;element_to_dict&#96; function"

            "text": "        :return: A percentage score of how similar is the candidate to the original element"

            "text": "        \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 810,
            "text": "        score: float = 0"

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 812,
            "text": "        data = _StorageTools.element_to_dict(candidate)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 813
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 814,
            "text": "        score += 1 if original[\"tag\"] == data[\"tag\"] else 0"

          {
            "lineNumber": 815,
            "text": "        checks += 1"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 816
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 817,
            "text": "        if original[\"text\"]:"
          },
          {

            "text": "            checks += 1"

          {
            "lineNumber": 820
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 821,
            "text": "        # if both don't have attributes, it still counts for something!"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 822,
            "text": "        score += self.__calculate_dict_diff(original[\"attributes\"], data[\"attributes\"])"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 823,
            "text": "        checks += 1"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 824
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 825,
            "text": "        # Separate similarity test for class, id, href,... this will help in full structural changes"
          }

            "text": "class Selector(SelectorsGeneration):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 945,
            "text": "def re_first("

            "lineNumber": 946,

            "text": "        case_sensitive: bool = True,"
          },

            "text": "    ) -\u003e TextHandler:"

          {

          {
            "lineNumber": 962
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 963,
            "text": "    @staticmethod"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 964,
            "text": "    def __get_attributes(element: HtmlElement, ignore_attributes: List | Tuple) -\u003e Dict:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 965,
            "text": "        \"\"\"Return attributes dictionary without the ignored list\"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 966,
            "text": "        return {k: v for k, v in element.attrib.items() if k not in ignore_attributes}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 967
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 968,
            "text": "    def __are_alike("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 969,
            "text": "        self,"
          },

            "text": "        original: HtmlElement,"
          },

            "text": "        original_attributes: Dict,"

            "text": "        candidate: HtmlElement,"

            "text": "        similarity_threshold: float,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 975,
            "text": "        match_text: bool = False,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 976,
            "text": "    ) -\u003e bool:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 977,
            "text": "        \"\"\"Calculate a score of how much these elements are alike and return True"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 978,
            "text": "        if the score is higher or equals the threshold\"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 979,
            "text": "        candidate_attributes = ("

          {
            "lineNumber": 980,
            "text": "            self.__get_attributes(candidate, ignore_attributes) if ignore_attributes else candidate.attrib"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 981,
            "text": "        )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 982,
            "text": "        score: float = 0"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 983,
            "text": "        checks: int = 0"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 984
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 985,
            "text": "        if original_attributes:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 986,
            "text": "            score += sum("

          {
            "lineNumber": 987,
            "text": "                SequenceMatcher(None, v, candidate_attributes.get(k, \"\")).ratio()"

          {

          },
          {

            "text": "            )"

          {

            "text": "            checks += len(candidate_attributes)"

          {
            "lineNumber": 991,
            "text": "        else:"

          {
            "lineNumber": 992,
            "text": "            if not candidate_attributes:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 993,
            "text": "                # Both don't have attributes, this must mean something"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 994,
            "text": "                score += 1"
          },

            "lineNumber": 995,
            "text": "                checks += 1"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 996
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 997,
            "text": "        if match_text:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 998,
            "text": "            score += SequenceMatcher("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 999,
            "text": "                None,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1000,
            "text": "                clean_spaces(original.text or \"\"),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1001,
            "text": "                clean_spaces(candidate.text or \"\"),"

            "text": "            checks += 1"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1004
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1005,
            "text": "        if checks:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1006,
            "text": "            return round(score / checks, 2) \u003e= similarity_threshold"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1007,
            "text": "        return False"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1008
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1009,
            "text": "    def find_similar("

          },

            "lineNumber": 564,
            "text": "    def css("

            "text": "        self,"

          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 571,
            "text": "    ) -\u003e \"Selectors\":"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 572,
            "text": "        \"\"\"Search the current tree with CSS3 selectors"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 573
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 574,
            "text": "        **Important:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 575,
            "text": "        It's recommended to use the identifier argument if you plan to use a different selector later"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 576,
            "text": "        and want to relocate the same element(s)**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 577
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 578,
            "text": "        :param selector: The CSS3 selector to be used."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 579,
            "text": "        :param adaptive: Enabled will make the function try to relocate the element if it was 'saved' before"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 580,
            "text": "        :param identifier: A string that will be used to save/retrieve element's data in adaptive,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 581,
            "text": "         otherwise the selector will be used."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 582,
            "text": "        :param auto_save: Automatically save new elements for &#96;adaptive&#96; later"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 583,
            "text": "        :param percentage: The minimum percentage to accept while &#96;adaptive&#96; is working and not going lower than that."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 584,
            "text": "         Be aware that the percentage calculation depends solely on the page structure, so don't play with this"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 585,
            "text": "         number unless you must know what you are doing!"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 586
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 587,
            "text": "        :return: &#96;Selectors&#96; class."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 588,
            "text": "        \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 589,
            "text": "        if self._is_text_node(self._root):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 590,
            "text": "            return Selectors()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 591
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 592,
            "text": "        try:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 593,
            "text": "            if not self.__adaptive_enabled or \",\" not in selector"
          }
        ],

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 1196,
            "text": "class Selectors(List[Selector]):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1197,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1198,
            "text": "    The &#96;Selectors&#96; class is a subclass of the builtin &#96;&#96;List&#96;&#96; class, which provides a few additional methods."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1199,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1200
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1201,
            "text": "    __slots__ = ()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1202
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1203,
            "text": "    @overload"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1204,
            "text": "    def __getitem__(self, pos: SupportsIndex) -\u003e Selector:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1205,
            "text": "        pass"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1206
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1207,
            "text": "    @overload"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1208,
            "text": "    def __getitem__(self, pos: slice) -\u003e \"Selectors\":"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1209,
            "text": "        pass"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1210
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1211,
            "text": "    def __getitem__(self, pos: SupportsIndex | slice) -\u003e Union[Selector, \"Selectors\"]:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1212,
            "text": "        lst = super().__getitem__(pos)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1213,
            "text": "        if isinstance(pos, slice):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1214,
            "text": "            return self.__class__(cast(List[Selector], lst))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1215,
            "text": "        else:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1216,
            "text": "            return cast(Selector, lst)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1217
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1218,
            "text": "    def xpath("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1219,
            "text": "        self,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1220,
            "text": "        selector: str,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1221,
            "text": "        identifier: str = \"\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1222,
            "text": "        auto_save: bool = False,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1223,
            "text": "        percentage: int = 0,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1224,
            "text": "        **kwargs: Any,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1225,
            "text": "    ) -\u003e \"Selectors\":"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1226,
            "text": "        \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1227,
            "text": "        Call the &#96;&#96;.xpath()&#96;&#96; method for each element in this list and return"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1228,
            "text": "        their results as another &#96;Selectors&#96; class."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1229
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1230,
            "text": "        **Important:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1231,
            "text": "        It's recommended to use the identifier argument if you plan to use a different selector later"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1232,
            "text": "        and want to relocate the same element(s)**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1233
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1234,
            "text": "         Note: **Additional keyword arguments will be passed as XPath variables in the XPath expression!**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1235
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1236,
            "text": "        :param selector: The XPath selector to be used."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1237,
            "text": "        :param identifier: A string that will be used to retrieve element's data in adaptive,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1238,
            "text": "         otherwise the selector will be used."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1239,
            "text": "        :param auto_save: Automatically save new elements for &#96;adaptive&#96; later"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1240,
            "text": "        :param percentage: The minimum percentage to accept while &#96;adaptive&#96; is working and not going lower than that."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1241,
            "text": "         Be aware that the percentage calculation depends solely on the page structure, so don't play with this"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1242,
            "text": "         number unless you must know what you are doing!"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1243
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1244,
            "text": "        :return: &#96;Selectors&#96; class."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1245,
            "text": "        \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1246,
            "text": "        results = [n.xpath(selector, identifier or selector, False, auto_save, percentage, **kwargs) for n in self]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1247,
            "text": "        return self.__class__(flatten(results))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1248
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1249,
            "text": "    def css("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1250,
            "text": "        self,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1251,
            "text": "        selector: str,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1252,
            "text": "        identifier: str = \"\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1253,
            "text": "        auto_save: bool = False,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1254,
            "text": "        percentage: int = 0,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1255,
            "text": "    ) -\u003e \"Selectors\":"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1256,
            "text": "        "
          }

            "text": "class Selector(SelectorsGeneration):"

            "text": "def save(self, element: HtmlElement, identifier: str) -\u003e None:"

            "text": "\""

          {
            "lineNumber": 897
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 898,
            "text": "    def retrieve(self, identifier: str) -\u003e Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 899,
            "text": "        \"\"\"Using the identifier, we search the storage and return the unique properties of the element"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 900
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 901,
            "text": "        :param identifier: This is the identifier that will be used to retrieve the element from the storage. See"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 902,
            "text": "            the docs for more info."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 903,
            "text": "        :return: A dictionary of the unique properties"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 904,
            "text": "        \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 905,
            "text": "        if self.__adaptive_enabled and self._storage:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 906,
            "text": "            return self._storage.retrieve(identifier)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 907
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 908,
            "text": "        raise RuntimeError("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 909,
            "text": "            \"Can't use &#96;adaptive&#96; features while it's disabled globally, you have to start a new class instance.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 910,
            "text": "        )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 911
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 912,
            "text": "    # Operations on text functions"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 913,
            "text": "    def json(self) -\u003e Dict:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 914,
            "text": "        \"\"\"Return JSON response if the response is jsonable otherwise throws error\"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 915,
            "text": "        if self._is_text_node(self._root):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 916,
            "text": "            return TextHandler(str(self._root)).json()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 917,
            "text": "        if self._raw_body and isinstance(self._raw_body, (str, bytes)):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 918,
            "text": "            if isinstance(self._raw_body, str):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 919,
            "text": "                return TextHandler(self._raw_body).json()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 920,
            "text": "            else:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 921,
            "text": "                if TYPE_CHECKING:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 922,
            "text": "                    assert isinstance(self._raw_body, bytes)"

          {

            "text": "                return TextHandler(self._raw_body.decode()).json()"
          },
          {

          {

            "text": "            return self.text.json()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 926,
            "text": "        else:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 927,
            "text": "            return self.get_all_text(strip=True).json()"

          {
            "lineNumber": 928
          },

          },

          {
            "isSignature": true,

            "text": "class SelectorsGeneration:"

            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "def _general_selection(self: Any, selection: str = \"css\", full_path: bool = False) -\u003e str:"

          {
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "        while target is not None:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "            if target.parent:"
          },

            "text": "                    # id is enough"
          },
          {

            "text": "                    part = f\"#{target.attrib['id']}\" if css else f\"[@id='{target.attrib['id']}']\""

          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "                    if not full_path:"
          },

          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "                    part = f\"{target.tag}\""

          {

          {

          },
          {

            "text": "                    # else:"
          },
          {

            "text": "                    counter: Dict[str, int] = {}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "                    for child in target.parent.children:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "                        counter.setdefault(child.tag, 0)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "                        counter[child.tag] += 1"

          {

          },
          {

            "text": "                            break"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "                    if counter[target.tag] \u003e 1:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "                        part += f\":nth-of-type({counter[target.tag]})\" if css else f\"[{counter[target.tag]}]\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "                selectorPath.append(part)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51,
            "text": "                target = target.parent"
          },
          {

            "text": "                if target is None or target.tag == \"html\":"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": "                    return \" \u003e \".join(reversed(selectorPath)) if css else \"//\" + \"/\".join(reversed(selectorPath))"

          {
            "lineNumber": 54,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55,
            "text": "                break"

          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57,
            "text": "        return \" \u003e \".join(reversed(selectorPath)) if css else \"//\" + \"/\".join(reversed(selectorPath))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59,
            "text": "    @property"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": "    def generate_css_selector(self: Any) -\u003e str:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61,
            "text": "        \"\"\"Generate a CSS selector for the current element"
